title: Teline candicans
intro: Teline candicans, also known as Star of Bethlehem or Ornithogalum umbellatum, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. Its roots and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Teline candicans include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Teline candicans is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried root, and is used to treat various ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Teline candicans is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the bush lupin. The classification of Teline candicans is as follows: it belongs to the genus Teline and the family Fabaceae. Teline candicans is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Genisteae. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region.
uses: Teline candicans, also known as the golden broom, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
